title: pentacalia pulchella subsp. guantivana
intro: Pentacalia pulchella subsp. guantivana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the cloud forests of Ecuador. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic, which can help to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, Pentacalia pulchella subsp. guantivana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
